body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable
driver_capability: ready

(#476 re-derivation of the prior `irrlicht_observes: partial`. The old `partial` conflated a fully-observable half with a structurally-unobservable half; splitting onto the two axes: the blocking fact is a spec-required signal that leaves NO trace in the watched Source — `daemon_capability: incapable`, NOT `bug` — while the driver has every step the recipe needs — `driver_capability: ready`.)

## Reasoning

### agent_supports: yes

The token-accounting primitive is *token reporting*: per-turn token counts (input, output, cache read/write) in a field the parser can extract, requiring `headless_mode` (`replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` → `headless_mode: true`). Aider satisfies both halves of the scenario at the agent layer:

1. **Per-turn counts that accumulate.** After every model call aider prints `Tokens: <sent> sent, <received> received.` and persists a `> Tokens: …` blockquote to `.aider.chat.history.md` in the project CWD (https://aider.chat/docs/faq.html). Three back-to-back one-message turns yield three such lines, each carrying that call's real usage — exactly the multi-turn accumulator the scenario locks down.
2. **Cache buckets exist at the agent layer.** With `--cache-prompts` (and `--no-stream`, since provider APIs don't surface cache stats while streaming — https://aider.chat/docs/usage/caching.html), aider additionally reports cache segments. `calculate_and_show_tokens_and_cost` in `aider/coders/base_coder.py` builds the report conditionally: `"Tokens: {sent} sent"`, then `", {cache_write} cache write"` if `cache_write_tokens`, then `", {cache_hit} cache hit"` if `cache_hit_tokens`, then `", {received} received."` — so a fully-cached turn shows e.g. `Tokens: 21k sent, 19k cache hit, 964 received` (https://github.com/Aider-AI/aider/blob/main/aider/coders/base_coder.py).

### daemon_capability: incapable

The aider adapter is `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", Parser: RawLineParser}` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/agent.go`). irrlicht observes only what lands in that markdown history. Judging the named User-observable signals against the parser as it exists today splits cleanly into an observable half and a blind half — and the blind half is one of the scenario's own User-observable signals, with no trace in the Source. That is `incapable` (architecture: no trace), not `bug` (which would require a trace the daemon mis-handles).

**Observable half — the multi-turn accumulator (the scenario's primary assertion):** `TotalTokens > 0` after turn 1, non-decreasing after turn 2 is fully met. Each `> Tokens:` line matches `tokensRE` and routes through `closeModelCall` (`parser.go`), emitting an `assistant_message` carrying `PerTurnContribution{Usage:{Input:sent, Output:received}}` and `TokenSnapshot{Input, Output, Total: sent+received}`. The cumulative accumulator in `core/pkg/tailer/tailer_metrics.go` sums per-turn contributions, so `TotalTokens` rises monotonically across turns. `parser_test.go` (`TestParser_MultipleTokensLines`, `TestParser_MultiTurn_EmitsAssistantMessagePerTurn`) pins one `assistant_message` per `> Tokens:` line and monotonic accumulation across consecutive turns. The recorded cell's transcript carries the two `> Tokens: 2.5k sent, 1 received. Cost: $…` lines that drive this. (Note: per `scenarios.json`, this accumulator is observable in the parser's events but NOT expressible in the `expected.jsonl` phase DSL — it's covered out-of-band by those parser unit tests; the recording asserts only the lifecycle arc it rides on.)

**Blind half — the cache distinction (`CacheReadTokens > 0`; fresh input = 0 for a fully-cached turn) — NO TRACE in the Source:** Two independent walls, the first of which is decisive for `incapable`:

  1. **Transport: no trace.** Aider's cache-bucket report is emitted via `self.io.tool_output(self.usage_report)` in `show_usage_report` — terminal only; it is never passed to `append_chat_history`. Every `> Tokens:` line that lands in `.aider.chat.history.md` is the bare `N sent, M received[. Cost: …]` form. Empirically, all 11 distinct `> Tokens:` lines across irrlicht's own recorded aider transcripts (incl. this cell's two) carry zero `cache hit` / `cache write` segments. Because the cache signal leaves no trace in the watched Source, no `FilesUnderCWD` watch and no parser can ever emit it — the `daemon_capability: incapable` definition exactly ("the behavior leaves no trace in the Source").
  2. **Parser: would-also-be-dropped.** Even if a cache segment did reach the markdown, `tokensRE` (`^>\s*Tokens:\s*([\d.]+\s*[kKmM]?)\s*sent,\s*([\d.]+\s*[kKmM]?)\s*received(.*)$`, `parser.go:58`) captures only `sent`/`received`; cache text falls into the unused `(.*)$` rest group, which `closeModelCall` scans only for a `$… message` cost suffix. `UsageBreakdown.CacheRead`/`CacheCreation` are never populated, so `metrics.CacheReadTokens` stays 0. This is a second-order observation, not the verdict driver — the trace already doesn't exist (wall 1), so the cell is `incapable` regardless. (`parser.go` carries the `CacheRead`/`CacheCreation` fields, ~lines 223-237; the aider parser leaves them zero.)

The scenario's core multi-turn accumulator is observed accurately; the cache-bucket sub-assertion — itself a named User-observable signal — is structurally unobservable through aider's file-watching transport. Routing on the blocking axis: a spec-required signal with no trace in the Source → `daemon_capability: incapable`.

### driver_capability: ready

The recorded recipe needs only `send` + `wait_turn` + `sleep`, all implemented by `scripts/drive-aider-interactive.sh` (tmux REPL driver; `wait_turn` blocks on a new `> Tokens:` line; keeps one aider session alive across all send/wait_turn cycles). No gapped primitive (`restart`/`reset_session`/`resume`/`keys`/`sigkill`/…) is required for token-accounting — `driver_capability: ready`.

## Routing note

With `daemon_capability: incapable`, the cell routes **frozen** — no recording could ever prove the cache distinction. In practice the maintainer has recorded the OBSERVABLE half (multi-turn accumulator) and explicitly carved the cache sub-assertion out of `verify` (`scenarios.json` → "NO cache-bucket assertion"); that recording documents what IS observable, while this assessment documents that the spec's cache signal is fundamentally unobservable for aider.

## Matrix

Refines the coverage cell (`agent-scenarios-coverage.json` → aider/token-accounting), which currently reads `daemon_capability: unknown`, `driver_capability: ready`, "regex-parsed '> Tokens: N sent, M received'; no cache-bucket distinction". This assessment resolves `unknown` → `incapable` (cache signal has no trace in the Source); `driver_capability: ready` unchanged; `agent_supports: yes` unchanged.

Primitive → capability key: the primitive is *token reporting*; the matching key in `replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` is `cost_tracking: false`, which is consistent — aider carries sent/received token counts but no per-bucket cache accounting reaching the transcript.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: aider's cache-bucket usage report (`Tokens: … cache write … cache hit …`) is written via io.tool_output to the terminal only — never appended to .aider.chat.history.md — so irrlicht's file-watching transport never sees it. The multi-turn token accumulator IS fully observed; only the cache distinction is unobservable.

Even if a cache segment reached the markdown, the parser's tokensRE captures only sent/received; closeModelCall never sets UsageBreakdown.CacheRead/CacheCreation, so CacheReadTokens stays 0. Closing this would need BOTH a transcript-level cache line AND a regex/parser change — but the transport gap (no trace) is the binding one.

aider's cache-hit tokens are reported separately from `sent` (sent stays non-zero on a cached turn), so the scenario's 'fresh input tokens = 0 for a fully-cached turn' assertion does not map onto aider's reporting shape even at the agent layer.

The token-accumulation claim (TotalTokens > 0 / non-decreasing) is NOT expressible in the expected.jsonl phase DSL; it is covered out-of-band by parser_test.go (TestParser_MultipleTokensLines, TestParser_MultiTurn_EmitsAssistantMessagePerTurn). The recording asserts only the two-turn lifecycle arc the accounting rides on.

sources.note: --cache-prompts enables provider prompt caching; cache statistics/costs unavailable while streaming, so --no-stream is required to surface them

calculate_and_show_tokens_and_cost builds 'Tokens: {sent} sent[, {cache_write} cache write][, {cache_hit} cache hit], {received} received.'; show_usage_report emits it via io.tool_output (terminal) — NOT append_chat_history. Proves the cache report leaves no trace in the transcript (daemon_capability: incapable).
aider writes per-project chat history to .aider.chat.history.md including the `> Tokens:` blockquote lines (the observable multi-turn accumulator)

Source = FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser} — irrlicht observes only what lands in the markdown history, not terminal output; grounds the no-trace / daemon_capability: incapable verdict for the cache signal

tokensRE (line 58) captures sent/received only (cache text falls in the unused rest group); closeModelCall sets UsageBreakdown.Input/Output + TokenSnapshot{Input,Output,Total} but never CacheRead/CacheCreation; one assistant_message per `> Tokens:`. Even with a trace the parser would drop the cache buckets.

accumulates per-turn contributions into cumulative TotalTokens (monotonic across turns — the observable half is full); CumCacheReadTokens sourced from UsageBreakdown.CacheRead, which aider never populates → stays 0

TestParser_MultipleTokensLines / TestParser_MultiTurn_EmitsAssistantMessagePerTurn pin one assistant_message per `> Tokens:` line and monotonic accumulation across turns; every fixture uses the bare `N sent, M received.` form — no cache segment

recorded transcript carries two `> Tokens: 2.5k sent, 1 received. Cost: $…` lines (no cache segment) — empirical confirmation the cache report never reaches .aider.chat.history.md; together with the other 9 distinct recorded aider `> Tokens:` lines, zero carry a cache hit/write segment
tmux REPL driver implements send + wait_turn + sleep (wait_turn blocks on a new `> Tokens:` line); the token-accounting recipe needs only these — no gapped primitive → driver_capability: ready
headless_mode: true (primitive's required capability); cost_tracking: false — token counts present, no per-bucket cache accounting reaches the transcript
